exemplar.title: To what extent has the conflict between HYBE Entertainment and Ador Entertainment impacted HYBEâs corporate governance, financial performance, and stakeholder relationships?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the conflict between HYBE Entertainment and Ador Entertainment impacted HYBEâs corporate governance, financial performance, and stakeholder relationships?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and consistent framing around ethics throughout most sections
Explicit linkage of Kantian, deontological and virtue ethics in stakeholder analysis
Frequent ethical terminology anchoring discussion to the key concept
weaknesses: Integration occasionally lapses into purely strategic commentary without ethical tie-in (e.g., Porterâs forces)
Introduction lacks an upfront ethical framing
Ethical contrasts (e.g., virtue ethics outcomes) could be deepened
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 4: a strong understanding of ethics that is threaded through most of the IA.  Occasional generic sections prevent full, seamless integration, so 4/5 accurately reflects performance.

strengths: Exactly five documents meet the 3â5 requirement
All sources are directly relevant to the HYBEâADOR dispute
weaknesses: Over-reliance on popular press; no primary financial statements or triangulated market reports
Limited depth and range of perspectives
Key quantitative data drawn from a single article
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission provides the correct number of documents (band 3) but they lack depth and triangulation (band 2).  A score of 2/4 reflects general relevance without sufficient analytical richness.

strengths: Use of three distinct frameworks (stakeholder matrix, Porterâs Five Forces, Aakerâs model)
Each tool is at least tied back to aspects of the research question
weaknesses: Selection omits key financial tools that would illuminate the share-price decline
Application often remains descriptive before brief HYBE linkage
Methodology section is incomplete, undermining clarity of approach
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although appropriate tools are chosen, their breadth and depth are limited and some methodological gaps persist.  This aligns most closely with band 2 for superficial selection and relevance, hence 2/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Includes quantitative evidence such as the 7.81% share-price drop and fan-protest metrics
Attempts comparison with SM Entertainment for strategic perspective
weaknesses: Data range is narrow and repetitive; analysis feels episodic
Several subsections end mid-analysis without completion
Limited triangulation or varied metrics to deepen evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of data is more than non-existent, but its superficiality and episodic treatment place the work in band 2 (superficial selection and use of data and limited analysis), warranting 2/5.

strengths: Restates key findings on governance risks, financial impact and stakeholder trust
Conclusions align generally with the evidence presented
weaknesses: Does not quantify âto what extentâ across focal areas
Minimal engagement with counter-evidence (e.g., NewJeansâ continued sales)
Lacks structured subsections or quantitative summaries
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion offers a coherent summary and is consistent with evidence (band 2), but stops short of an explicit, nuanced answer to the research question.  Thus it merits 2/3.

strengths: Logical sequence of introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion
Clear table of contents and headings
weaknesses: Table of contents lists a matrix not present in the text
Abrupt transitions between sections weaken flow
Research question not restated verbatim in the conclusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is an underlying organizational logic, mismatches in the ToC and abrupt shifts indicate only limited structural coherence, corresponding to band 1.  Score: 1/2.

strengths: Includes all required elements: title page, word count, bibliography, appendices
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting (missing years/page numbers)
Semicolon used instead of colon in the Research Question label
Appendix material is embedded in findings rather than clearly signposted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite inclusion of all components, recurring formatting and citation errors compromise presentation quality.  This limited adherence aligns with band 1.  Score: 1/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical sequence (introduction, methodology, tools, evaluation, conclusion) and includes a table of contents and headings/sub-headings.  Transitions are occasionally abrupt, but overall organisation is appropriate for an IA, satisfying the top band for this two-mark criterion.

All documents relate to the HYBEâADOR dispute and provide basic quantitative or qualitative evidence (e.g., stock fall, fan protests).  However, they derive almost exclusively from popular press sources; depth is limited and little triangulation is offered.  This shows general relevance but lacks the in-depth or wide-ranging insight of higher bands, so band 2 is appropriate.

The submission contains all required elements: title page with word count, table of contents, bibliography, appendices and in-text citations.  Therefore it earns the full marks available for presentation.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed and referenced, satisfying the rubricâs 3-5 requirement.  Therefore the submission meets the criteria for this middle band.

Each tool is applied to the research question with some specificityâe.g., mapping HYBE, ADOR and fans in the stakeholder matrix and linking Porterâs forces to reputational fallout.  Yet much of the discussion remains descriptive (explaining what the force is) before briefly relating it to HYBE; numerical data are rarely embedded within the frameworks.  Application is therefore âadequateâ but the relevance is not consistently clear or deeply analysed, fitting band 3.

The student cites quantitative evidence (share-price graph, market-cap loss) and qualitative statements from press articles, demonstrating more than a âsuperficialâ use of data.  However, the range is narrow and largely repetitive (same 7.81 % figure appears twice).  While sufficient for basic analysis, the selection is not deep or varied enough to merit the higher bands; hence âadequateâ (band 3).

The analysis moves beyond description by identifying ethical tensions (Friedman vs Freeman) and drawing links between governance actions and stakeholder reactions.  Nonetheless, evaluation is episodicâPorterâs forces and the Aaker model are only partly interpreted, and counter-arguments or depth of synthesis are limited.  Consequently the work meets the âsome analysisâ descriptor but falls short of âmostly effectiveâ integration, aligning with band 3.

The research question asks âto what extentâ. The conclusion asserts that resolving the conflict is âcriticalâ, implying material impact, but it stops short of a nuanced judgement of extent across governance, finance and relationships.  Thus the answer is more than superficial yet not fully explicit or balanced, meriting the partial-answer band 2.

Three distinct tools (Stakeholder matrix, Porterâs Five Forces, Aakerâs Brand Equity model) are appropriate for exploring governance, competitive position and brand impact.  This represents an âadequate selectionâ; nevertheless, the investigation omits common financial tools (ratio, trend or break-even analysis) that could illuminate the 7.81 % share-price decline, so the choice is not âeffectiveâ in breadth or precision.

The conclusion restates key findings (impact on governance, finance, stakeholder trust) and generally aligns with evidence such as the share-price drop and fan backlash, indicating basic consistency.  However, it glosses over contrary evidence (e.g., continued strong NewJeans sales) and provides minimal quantitative synthesis, so consistency is partial rather than full, fitting band 2.

The conflict is thoroughly situated in HYBEâs real-world context: share-price data, ownership percentages, subsidiary structure and fan protests are all connected to each analytical tool.  The organisationâs governance model, revenue reliance on NewJeans and market position are woven into most sections.  Nonetheless, occasional paragraphs become generic (e.g., generic definitions of Porterâs forces) without tying back to HYBE, so the integration is strong but not âcomprehensiveâ.  Hence band 4 is most appropriate.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the key concept of ethics, explicitly linking the HYBEâADOR dispute to Kantian, deontological, virtue and shareholder-vs-stakeholder ethical theories. Ethical language recurs in the stakeholder matrix, Porter's analysis and the conclusion, demonstrating a solid conceptual grasp.  However, integration is not seamless across every section; portions of the Porter and Brand Equity discussions revert to purely strategic commentary with minimal ethical linkage.  This places the work above âclear analytical thinkingâ but short of the âfully integrated throughoutâ standard, warranting the strong-but-not-comprehensive band 4.

annotations.comment: The explicit identification of âEthicsâ as the key concept provides a strong conceptual anchor for the entire study, clearly framing the investigation.
The research question is clearly stated, encompassing governance, financial performance, and stakeholder relationships, which guides a multifaceted analysis.
The label âResearch Question;â uses a semicolon instead of a colon. Revise to âResearch Question:â to align with academic conventions and improve clarity.
The table of contents provides a detailed roadmap, enhancing navigation through the reportâs structure.
The introduction provides background but lacks an explicit mention of ethics to frame the conflict within the key concept from the outset.
The methodology section ends abruptly midâsentence (âTheâ), leaving the research approach underâexplained. Complete this section and clarify data selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Plot stakeholders explicitly on the matrix with justifications for their power and interest levels to enhance analytical rigor.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The ethical discussion could be deepened by contrasting outcomes under virtue ethics for each stakeholder group.
The table of contents lists an âAnsoff Matrixâ that does not appear in the main text. Ensure the ToC matches actual headings to avoid reader confusion.
Several inâtext citations omit publication years or page numbers. Standardize citation formatting (e.g., APA or Harvard) to improve academic rigor.
Expand the methodology to detail how ethical frameworks were chosen, data sources were evaluated, and triangulation ensured balance.
Supporting Document 1 quantifies the 7.81% shareâprice drop, effectively demonstrating a tangible financial impact.
The analysis relies on a single news article for all quantitative data. Incorporate primary financial statements or market reports to enhance depth.
The repeated citation of a 7.81% drop in multiple sections creates redundancy and limits insight. Introduce varied metrics or time frames.
Consider adding marketâshare figures, revenue growth rates, or profitability ratios to diversify the data set and strengthen analysis.
Appendix Vâs full narrative is embedded in the findings rather than summarized. Extract key points and refer to the appendix for detail.
Summarize appendix findings in the main text and direct readers to the full data in the appendix to maintain flow.
The PowerâInterest matrix is described generically without plotted stakeholder positions. Embed the actual matrix with labels and coordinates.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The application of Kantian and deontological ethics in stakeholder analysis demonstrates strong integration of the key concept.
The heading â2. Brand Awarenessâ appears but is not developed. Provide evidence and analysis for this dimension.
The âThreat of Substitutesâ subsection ends midâanalysis. Complete the evaluation to explain how substitutes impact HYBEâs position.
Support each force with dataâcompetitor market shares, fan surveys, or partnership trendsâto lend empirical weight to your assessments.
The dual presentation of YouTube view metrics and fan protests offers balanced evidence of changes in brand loyalty.
The perceived quality section references a police report but doesnât link to consumer perceptions. Add survey or socialâmedia data to illustrate stakeholder sentiment.
The comparative evaluation with SM Entertainmentâs dispute management offers valuable strategic perspective.
The claim about SMâs mediation style lacks citation. Reference a source to substantiate this comparative insight.
The conclusion summarizes findings but does not quantify âto what extentâ across the three focal areas. Deepen the judgement by specifying degrees of impact.
Structure the conclusion with clear subsections for governance, finance, and stakeholder impacts, and where possible, include quantitative summaries.
The conclusion does not restate the research question verbatim, which can weaken alignment. Rearticulate the question to close the loop explicitly.
The conclusion effectively restates key analytic findingsâgovernance risks, stock impacts, and trust erosionâproviding a cohesive summary.
